specific_mechanism: BR-Couple: in red-team/aligned coevolution, compute Bellman residual R(Q_R) and R(Q_A) per training cycle. Coupling constraint |R(Q_R) - R(Q_A)| ≤ τ; if violated, shrink η of dominant agent by 0.5.
llm_application: BR-Couple: at each coevolution outer loop, fit a small Q estimator per agent (red-team Q_R, aligned Q_A) on rollouts; compute Bellman residual norm ‖T Q - Q‖₂ via TD error sum. If |R(Q_R)-R(Q_A)|>τ (default 0.2), shrink the dominant agent's learning rate by factor 0.5 next cycle. Otherwise both step normally.
novelty_claim: AdvEvo-MARL (2510.01586) uses adversarial coevolution; Robust MARL adversarial regularization (2310.10810). Bellman-residual-gap coupling as adversarial coevolution constraint specifically is less common.
motivation_justification: Bellman residual is a precise algebraic quantity; gap between two agents' residuals is a well-defined coupling.
sub_mechanisms: M_2: Bellman residual ‖T Q - Q‖ computation
content_words: dominant agent learning rate shrink adapter
RLHF safety red team multi-agent training
